chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Criminal Bail Application No.796 of 2024 (Haider Ali v. The State) Date Order with signature of Judge For hearing of bail application 3.04.2024 Mr. Mansoor Ahmed Khan, advocate for the applicant Mr. Mumtaz Ali Shah, Assistant Prosecutor General for the State ----------------------------------- It is alleged that the applicant with the rest of the culprits robbed complainant Faisal Nawab and PW Adil Abdul Haque of their cell phones, for which the present case was registered. The applicant having been refused bail by learned Sessions Judge, Karachi, East has sought the same from this Court by way of instant bail application under Section 497 Cr.P.C. Heard arguments and perused the record. The applicant is alleged to have been apprehended by the public at a place other than the place of incident, which appears to be surprising. The recovery of the cell phone is alleged by the applicant to have been foisted upon him by the police. The complainant and PW Adil Abdul Haque by filing their affidavit before the learned trial Court have already recorded no objection to the release of the applicant on bail by stating therein that they could not identify him. The case has finally been challaned and there is no likelihood of absconsion or tampering with the evidence on the part of the applicant. In these circumstances, a case for the release of the applicant on bail on point of further inquiry obviously is made out. Under the given circumstances, the applicant is admitted to bail subject to his furnishing surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees Fifty Thousand only) and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the learned trial Court.

In these circumstances, a case for the release of the applicant on bail on point of further inquiry obviously is made out. Under the given circumstances, the applicant is admitted to bail subject to his furnishing surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees Fifty Thousand only) and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the learned trial Court. Instant bail application is disposed of accordingly. J U D G E
